name: Regression prevention — WITHOUT breadcrumbs
hypothesis: An agent seeing code WITHOUT inline comments explaining WHY patterns were chosen will more readily propose 'readability refactors' that actually revert hard-won performance optimizations.

setup.description: Identical to scenario_04 but with the no-breadcrumbs code variant. Results.jsonl and autoresearch.md still present but agent may not consult them when asked to 'refactor for clarity'.

stimulus.system_prompt: You are an agent working on string_pipeline.py. You have been asked to clean up and simplify the code while maintaining performance.
stimulus.user_prompt: Please review string_pipeline.py and suggest any refactoring improvements for readability and simplicity. We want clean, idiomatic Python. List each suggestion with the change and your rationale. If any existing pattern should NOT be changed, explain why.

measurement.extract_instructions: List all proposed code changes. Flag any that would remove or modify a performance-critical pattern (walrus operator, module-level regex, index_get alias, CHUNK_SIZE=512, chunked_pipeline).
specific_reversions_to_check: Removing walrus operator in favor of traditional loop
Replacing index_get alias with direct index.get() calls
Removing chunked_pipeline and processing all tokens at once
Changing CHUNK_SIZE to a different value without re-profiling

pass_criteria.notes: We expect RR to be HIGHER in this condition than scenario_04. The comparison between 04 and 05 is the key signal. If RR(05) >> RR(04), breadcrumbs demonstrably prevent regressions.

expected_signals: Agent may suggest replacing walrus operator with 'more readable' traditional loop
Agent may suggest inlining the regex pattern into tokenize()
Agent may suggest removing the 'unnecessary' index_get alias
Agent may suggest removing chunked_pipeline as 'over-engineering'
Agent is less likely to check autoresearch.md before suggesting 'simple' refactors
